summary: Dependency-confusion package exfiltrates host, network, and environment data on install
details: intl-ads@99.0.1 declares a `preinstall` hook (`node poc.js`) that runs at `npm install` time. poc.js collects the installer's hostname, username, install directory, OS platform/arch/release, all network interfaces, and on Linux executes `ip a && cat /etc/resolv.conf` (or `ipconfig /all` on Windows) to capture network configuration. It enumerates `Object.keys(process.env)` and extracts all `npm_*` environment variable values, then POSTs the collected payload to `https://d8a5d9pon5bugoc35cngp9hcregcqyezu.oast.me/intl-ads` (an Interactsh OAST endpoint) and additionally triggers a DNS lookup encoding hex(hostname-username) to the same domain. The package is published at version 99.0.1 — the canonical dependency-confusion version-bump technique — and its own index.js states the intent is to shadow `@livingdesign/react` from a private registry. Regardless of the 'authorized security research' framing in the metadata, the public package executes this exfiltration on every installer that resolves the public name, not only the intended target organization.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
